meta.title: 5-SENSE Score — chance of a focal seizure-onset zone on SEEG
meta.authors: Astner-Rohracher A, Zimmermann G, Avigdor T, et al.
meta.outcome: Predicted probability that stereo-EEG (SEEG) will identify a FOCAL seizure-onset zone, from 5 non-invasive presurgical findings
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. The 5-SENSE score (Astner-Rohracher et al., JAMA Neurology 2022) estimates the probability that invasive stereo-EEG (SEEG) will reveal a focal seizure-onset zone, using five routinely available non-invasive findings. It helps decide whether SEEG is likely to be informative before implanting electrodes. Developed on 128 and externally validated on 207 patients (AUC 0.83).

How to read it

  • The score is the percent chance of a focal seizure-onset zone (0–100).
  • Above 37.6 → a focal zone is likely to be found on SEEG (favourable for focused implantation/resection). Below 37.6 → a focal zone is unlikely (sensitivity 83%, specificity 76%).
  • Strongest drivers: a focal MRI lesion and a focal/lobar ictal EEG onset raise the score; no lesion, bilateral-independent IEDs, or a diffuse ictal onset lower it.

Demonstration tool — an aid to presurgical discussion, not a substitute for the epilepsy surgery conference.

predictors.name: Lesion extent on MRI
predictors.assess: Extent of a lesion on presurgical MRI. A focal lesion strongly favours a focal seizure-onset zone; no lesion or a multilobar/extensive lesion argues against it.

predictors.name: Interictal EEG (IEDs) extent
predictors.assess: Extent of interictal epileptiform discharges (IEDs) on scalp EEG. 'No IEDs' and non–bilateral-independent patterns favour focality; bilateral-independent IEDs argue against it.

predictors.name: Neuropsychological deficit
predictors.assess: Localizing value of the neuropsychological deficit. A localizing deficit — or, paradoxically, no deficit — favours focality; a non-localizing (diffuse) deficit argues against it.

predictors.name: Localizing semiology
predictors.assess: Localizing value of the seizure semiology. Strongly localizing semiology favours a focal seizure-onset zone.

predictors.name: Ictal EEG onset extent
predictors.assess: Extent of the ictal EEG onset on scalp EEG. A focal/lobar onset favours focality; bilateral-synchronous or none/multilobar/diffuse onset argues against it.

model.panel_title: Chance of a focal seizure-onset zone

outputs.label: Chance of a focal seizure-onset zone (SEEG)

model.note: Score >37.6 → a focal seizure-onset zone is likely on SEEG; <37.6 → unlikely (optimal cutoff, Astner-Rohracher 2022).
model.viz_info: Each of the five findings adds or subtracts a weight (the published model coefficient). Their sum (plus a constant) is passed through a logistic function to give the percent chance of a focal seizure-onset zone. The waterfall shows how each finding pushes the estimate up or down; the donut shows the resulting probability.

provenance.source: Astner-Rohracher 2022 JAMA Neurol Fig 2A (coefficients) + Fig 3 (formula & worked examples) — PMID34870697, doi 10.1001/jamaneurol.2021.4405, PMC8649918
provenance.notes: Logistic model: score=100·exp(Σcoef−0.3135)/(1+exp(...)). Coefficients from Fig 2A; formula+constant−0.3135 & cutoff 37.6 from Fig 3. Verified vs both worked examples (patient A 0.61→57.4%, patient B −1.556→13.4%). Extracted 2026-07-07.
